title: 01.06 House of Assembly (Speaker and Other Members) (Salaries and Allowances) Ordinance

L C A A W IC S O O S F I T S U LA R N K D S S & (Salaries and Allowances) CAP. 1.06 5
Revision Date: 31 Mar 2021
CHAPTER 1.06
HOUSE OF ASSEMBLY
(SPEAKER AND OTHER MEMBERS)
(SALARIES AND ALLOWANCES) ORDINANCE
(Ordinances 41 of 2012, 4 of 2019 and 16 of 2019)
AN ORDINANCE TO PROVIDE FOR THE SALARIES, ALLOWANCES AND OTHER
BENEFITS OF MINISTERS, THE SPEAKER AND MEMBERS OF THE HOUSE OF
ASSEMBLY AND FOR CONNECTED PURPOSES.
Commencement
[9 November 2012]
Short title
1. This Ordinance may be cited as the House of Assembly (Speaker and
Other Members) (Salaries and Allowances) Ordinance.
Interpretation
2. In this Ordinance—
“all Islands district” means the single electoral district established by section
45(2)(a) of the Constitution; (Inserted by Ord. 16 of 2019)
“appointed member” means an appointed member of the House of Assembly;
“Constitution” means the Constitution set out in Schedule 2 to the Turks and
Caicos Constitution Order 2011 (S.I. 2011 No. 1681); (Inserted by Law
Revision Commissioner, Law Revision 2014)
“Deputy Speaker” means the member of the House of Assembly elected by the
House as Deputy Speaker under section 44 of the Constitution;
“elected member” means an elected member of the House of Assembly;
“Leader of the Opposition” means the person appointed as such under section 56
of the Constitution;
“Minister” means a person appointed as Premier or other Minister under section
31 of the Constitution;
“Premier” means the person appointed as such under section 31(2) of the
Constitution;
“Speaker” means the person elected by the House of assembly as Speaker under
section 44 of the Constitution.
Salaries and allowances of Ministers, etc.
3. (1) Subject to this Ordinance, there shall be paid to the persons holding
the offices and posts specified in the first column of the Schedule the salaries and
© Crown Copyright 2021
Copying/unauthorised distribution strictly prohibited.

House of Assembly
(Speaker and Other Members)
6 CAP. 1.06 (Salaries and Allowances) L C A A W IC S O O S F I T S U LA R N K D S S &
Revision Date: 31 Mar 2021
allowances respectively set forth opposite thereto in the second and third
columns of that Schedule.
(2) An elected member shall be paid a constituency allowance as
follows—
(a) a sum of $1,500 per month for a member elected from the all
Islands district; and
(b) a sum of $1,200 per month for any other elected member.
(Substituted by Ord. 16 of 2019)
Travel, subsistence and other expenses
4. (1) Subject to any rules or travel policy and procedures made in this
behalf by the Government, each Minister shall be reimbursed for travelling and
subsistence expenses incurred in respect of travel undertaken by him or her in
the discharge of their official duties.
(2) Subject to any rules or travel policy and procedure made in this behalf
by the Government, every member of the House of Assembly who does not hold
an office referred to in subsection (1) shall be reimbursed for travelling and
subsistence expenses incurred for attending the meetings of the House of
Assembly or any committee thereof or for the purpose of attending to any other
business connected with his or her duties as a member of the House.
(3) Subject to any rules, policy and procedures made in this behalf by the
Government, a member of the House of Assembly shall be reimbursed all out of
pocket expenses reasonably incurred in connection with or associated with a
member’s attendance to the House of Assembly or any committee thereof or for
the purpose of attending to any other business connected with his or her duties as
a member of the House. (Inserted by Ord. 16 of 2019)
Official vehicles
5. (1) The Government shall provide, by way of purchase or lease, official
vehicles for the use of Ministers on the Islands of Grand Turk and
Providenciales.
(2) The purchase or lease, as the case may be, of any vehicle pursuant to
subsection (1) shall be in accordance with the requirements of the Public
Procurement Ordinance.
(Substituted by Ord. 4 of 2019)
Transportation allowance
6. The Speaker shall be paid a transportation allowance of $500 per month.
(Inserted by Ord. 16 of 2019)

House of Assembly
(Speaker and Other Members)
L C A A W IC S O O S F I T S U LA R N K D S S & (Salaries and Allowances) CAP. 1.06 7
Revision Date: 31 Mar 2021
Housing allowance
7. (1) A Minister who ordinarily resides on the island of Grand Turk for
official duties, whose home base is not Grand Turk, shall be paid a housing
allowance of $3,000 per month.
(2) This section does not apply to the Premier.
(Inserted by Ord. 16 of 2019)
Acting allowance
8. (1) A Minister shall, when he or she is performing the functions of and
acting in the office of another Minister in a higher position to his or her office,
be paid an acting allowance for the period that he or she is performing such
functions and acting in that higher position.
(2) The acting allowance payable under subsection (1) shall be in
accordance with the policy applicable to acting allowances of a senior or
executive officer in the public service.
(Inserted by Ord. 16 of 2019)
Residence of Premier
9. (1) The Premier is entitled, without payment of rent, to the use of a
furnished and fully maintained (with the necessary household staff) official
residence on the islands of Providenciales and Grand Turk throughout his or her
term of office.
(2) Subject to subsection (3), the rental charges for the official residence
referred to in subsection (1) shall be—
(a) an amount that ranges from $10,000 and $15,000 per month for
the island of Providenciales; and
(b) not more than $6,000 per month for the island of Grand Turk.
(3) The rental charges under subsection (2) shall be determined and
approved by the Cabinet based upon a proper and credible evaluation report on
the relevant property.
(4) No charge shall fall on the Premier personally in respect of the
maintenance of an official residence.
(5) For the purposes of this section “maintenance” in relation to a
residence, includes the provision of electricity and water.
(Substituted by Ord. 16 of 2019)
Minister not to draw salary or allowances as member of the House
10. Subject to section 3(2), no member holding an office specified in the first
column of the Schedule shall be entitled to receive a salary or allowance by
virtue of being a member of the House of Assembly, in addition to that specified
for that office in the Schedule.
—————
